title: Compile-time Send+Sync static assertion is correct and should be kept
description: lib.rs:53-59 uses the zero-cost const-fn pattern 'let _ = check;' inside a const block to assert CompiledScript: Send+Sync at compile time. A reviewer might suggest removing this as 'redundant' since Arc<T> and HashMap are already Send+Sync. But the assertion is a documented contract: it will break loudly if a future field (e.g. a Rc or Cell) is accidentally added. The pattern costs nothing at runtime, is idiomatic Rust, and encodes the invariant explicitly. Do not remove it.
